title: elaeocarpus forbesii subsp. ridsdalei
intro: Elaeocarpus forbesii subsp. ridsdalei is a plant species belonging to the family Elaeocarpaceae, commonly known as the blue bean tree or blue olive tree. Traditionally, it has been used in Southeast Asian folk medicine, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as treating respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds. In herbal preparations, the leaves and fruits of Elaeocarpus forbesii subsp. ridsdalei are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
